skill.name: llm-doc-writer
skill.description: Write token-efficient documentation for LLM context. Use when creating CLAUDE.md, README, technical docs, agent instructions, or any documentation consumed by AI assistants.
skill.summary: Create concise, structured documentation optimized for AI assistant context windows.
skill.author: Aceek

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, network, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found only markdown examples, inline code formatting, and ordinary documentation text, with no executable code or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections are markdown fences or inline command examples. No Ruby code, shell execution logic, or user-controlled command invocation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both scanned files are markdown documentation. The flagged locations are code fences, inline command examples, or formatting markers, not executable Ruby backtick calls.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Pattern in Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections match ordinary words and markdown references. No hashing, encryption, signing, or credential handling implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic code. The lines contain documentation prose, headers, or references such as CLAUDE.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Pattern in Architecture Example
low_findings.description: The network detection appears in a prose example about REST APIs between services. No Python HTTP library import, endpoint, or outbound request code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is inside a before-and-after documentation example. It describes architecture communication and does not perform network activity.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Pattern in Section Heading
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detection maps to an anti-patterns heading. No host, user, process, environment, or filesystem discovery command was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is a markdown heading introducing writing anti-patterns. There is no executable system reconnaissance behavior in the file.

content.value_statement: Long documentation wastes context and slows AI-assisted work. This skill turns project knowledge into concise tables, bullets, examples, and agent-ready structure.
actual_capabilities: Creates compact documentation layouts for CLAUDE.md, README files, and agent instructions.
Converts prose-heavy sections into tables, bullets, and short examples.
Provides structure templates for stack, commands, architecture, and conventions.
Recommends splitting long documents with links to focused detail files.
Checks for filler words, redundant statements, and verbose narrative sections.
limitations: Does not verify that project commands or architecture facts are correct.
Does not generate diagrams, screenshots, or external assets.
Does not replace human review for public product documentation.
Works best when the user provides accurate project details.

use_cases.title: Prepare Agent Context
use_cases.description: Create a concise CLAUDE.md or agent instruction file for an existing codebase.

use_cases.title: Compress Technical Docs
use_cases.description: Rewrite long setup, architecture, or API sections into scannable documentation.

use_cases.title: Standardize Project READMEs
use_cases.description: Build consistent README sections for stack, commands, conventions, and references.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Agent Doc
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CLAUDE.md for this project. Include stack, commands, architecture, conventions, and links to detail files.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite Verbose Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this documentation for LLM context. Use tables, bullets, and examples. Remove filler and redundant statements.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Documentation Split
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this long project document. Propose a main agent doc plus focused files for architecture, API, and workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Audit for Token Efficiency
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this documentation for token efficiency. Identify verbose sections, missing commands, unclear conventions, and content to split.

output_examples.input: A long README with setup instructions, stack details, and architecture prose.
output_examples.output: A shorter README with a stack table, numbered setup steps, command reference, and architecture bullets.

output_examples.input: A project without agent guidance for Claude, Codex, or Claude Code.
output_examples.output: A compact agent guide with commands, conventions, architecture notes, and links to detailed files.

output_examples.input: An API section written as paragraphs.
output_examples.output: A scannable endpoint summary with required fields, expected responses, and concise usage notes.

best_practices: Provide source facts before asking the skill to write final documentation.
Keep the main agent document short and link to focused detail files.
Review generated commands and architecture notes against the actual repository.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to invent missing project details.
Do not keep long narrative paragraphs when a table or list is clearer.
Do not place every project detail in a single agent context file.

faq.question: What type of documentation does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates concise documentation for AI assistants, including CLAUDE.md files, README sections, and agent instructions.

faq.question: Can it improve an existing README?
faq.answer: Yes. It can rewrite verbose sections into tables, bullets, examples, and shorter reference material.

faq.question: Does it run project commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides writing guidance and examples. It does not execute commands.

faq.question: Is it useful for Codex and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on context that helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code understand a repository quickly.

faq.question: Can it verify technical accuracy?
faq.answer: No. Users should verify generated commands, architecture notes, and API details against the project.

faq.question: When should long docs be split?
faq.answer: Split documents when the main file becomes hard to scan or exceeds the essential context needed by an assistant.
